description: Emergent categories from Q3 responses (what changed operationally)
rule_0: All Q3 responses are one sentence, operational language only, no theoretical vocabulary

raw_q3_responses.q3: A classification scheme was applied to prior studies, separating them into two groups based on what remained stable.
Multiple sequences were generated and the same four features appeared in all of them.
Audits were classified by whether descriptions collapsed or were maintained, and gain was compared.
Comparisons were classified by type and collapse rates were measured.
One world was represented in multiple ways and the same detectors were applied to each.
Each junction was removed and the effect on the others was measured.
Removal fraction was varied and recovery metrics were measured.
Box width was varied and the effect on packing structure and outcome was measured.
Each junction was expressed as an operator and composition was tested.
The study sequence was re-read and the timing of gain relative to interaction was recorded.
Universes were generated with continuous dynamics and the emergence of separable clusters was measured.
Universes were generated and detectors were applied to determine which appeared first.
Removal fraction was varied and recovery metrics were measured.
Universes were analyzed for proximity structure and the chain was tested.
Pre-perturbation level was measured and its predictive power for recovery was tested.
Worlds were generated without pattern vocabulary and patterns were detected after evolution.
A co-occurrence matrix was built, noise was filtered, and clustering was applied.
Reconstruction was tested under four degradation conditions and breakpoints were identified.
Ten candidates were tested and all required separation.
Audits were decomposed by type and the contribution of each component was analyzed.

emergent_categories.description: Studies that classified or categorized existing data into groups
emergent_categories.pattern: Applied a scheme to separate items into groups based on shared properties

emergent_categories.description: Studies that generated new instances and tested whether patterns appeared
emergent_categories.pattern: Created new instances and measured whether specific features emerged

emergent_categories.description: Studies that varied a parameter and measured the effect on outcomes
emergent_categories.pattern: Systematically varied an input and measured changes in outputs

emergent_categories.description: Studies that removed components or degraded conditions to test robustness
emergent_categories.pattern: Removed or degraded components to test whether function persisted

emergent_categories.description: Studies that compared multiple descriptions or reinterpreted existing data
emergent_categories.pattern: Compared multiple descriptions or reinterpreted data through a new lens

sorted_by_frequency.what_changed: Universes/worlds were generated and patterns/features were measured
Items were classified/categorized into groups based on shared properties
A parameter was varied and the effect on outcomes was measured
Multiple descriptions were compared or reinterpreted through a new lens
Components were removed or degraded to test whether function persisted
